summary: See clues file for full details. 5 tossups, 12 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 5 tossups and 12 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: An egg hanging above a figure who is in this position inspired Salvador Dali to interpret that figure with a rectangular hole for a chest.
examples: An egg hanging above a figure who is in this position inspired Salvador Dali to interpret that figure with a rectangular hole for a chest.

clues.clue: An armor-clad Federico da Montefeltro kneels before a figure in this position in that sacra conversazione by Piero della Francesca.
examples: An armor-clad Federico da Montefeltro kneels before a figure in this position in that sacra conversazione by Piero della Francesca.

clues.clue: Simone Martini painted a red and blue canopy above a central figure in this position.
examples: Simone Martini painted a red and blue canopy above a central figure in this position.

clues.clue: This positioning of a figure is emphasized with liberal use of gold striations on a blue robe to create depth in a (*) Cimabue  painting.
examples: This positioning of a figure is emphasized with liberal use of gold striations on a blue robe to create depth in a (*) Cimabue  painting.

clues.clue: Giotto neglected that technique for chiaroscuro in the Ognissanti Madonna.
examples: Giotto neglected that technique for chiaroscuro in the Ognissanti Madonna.

clues.clue: While holding the infant Jesus, a madonna in this position forms the Maesta scene.
examples: While holding the infant Jesus, a madonna in this position forms the Maesta scene.

clues.clue: Marilyn Aronberg Lavin argued that a painting by this artist symbolizes the position of the sun on the winter solstice by positioning a dove and Christ's hands and feet using Euclid's formula for superimposing a pentagon on a triangle.
examples: Marilyn Aronberg Lavin argued that a painting by this artist symbolizes the position of the sun on the winter solstice by positioning a dove and Christ's hands and feet using Euclid's formula for superimposing a pentagon on a triangle.

clues.clue: Until the 19th century, this artist was better known as a mathematician because of works like the Abacus Treatise and On the Perspective of Painting, the first Renaissance treatise solely dedicated to perspective.
examples: Until the 19th century, this artist was better known as a mathematician because of works like the Abacus Treatise and On the Perspective of Painting, the first Renaissance treatise solely dedicated to perspective.

clues.clue: Martin Kemp noted the "geometric control" of a painting by this artist, contrasting the octagonal pattern of the tiles inside the (*) loggia with the simpler grid pattern of the tiles outside the loggia.
examples: Martin Kemp noted the "geometric control" of a painting by this artist, contrasting the octagonal pattern of the tiles inside the (*) loggia with the simpler grid pattern of the tiles outside the loggia.

clues.clue: The vanishing point of that painting by this artist is unusually low, near the hem of the robe of the man performing the title action, instead of on Christ's head.
examples: The vanishing point of that painting by this artist is unusually low, near the hem of the robe of the man performing the title action, instead of on Christ's head.

clues.clue: The Institute for Advanced Studies hosts a 3-D online walkthrough of this artist's fresco cycle in Arezzo, The History of the True Cross.
examples: The Institute for Advanced Studies hosts a 3-D online walkthrough of this artist's fresco cycle in Arezzo, The History of the True Cross.
